title: Contributing to resmush
text: outlines propose change resmush. detailed discussion contributing tidyverse packages, please see development contributing guide code review principles.

title: Contributing to resmush
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to resmush
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to resmush
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("dieghernan/resmush", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to resmush
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use Air apply style, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to resmush
text: contributing guide adapted tidyverse contributing guide available https://tidyverse.tidyverse.org/CONTRIBUTING.html.

title: Using resmush
text: Optimize download online JPEG image resmush_url(): () (b) Figure 1: Original image Figure 1 (): 178.7 KB, optimized image Figure 1 (b): 45 KB (compression: 74.8%). Click enlarge. Use qlty argument adjust JPEG quality level. best results, use values 90.  Figure 2: Image visible compression artifacts caused high compression (qlty = 3), compared Figure 1 (b). results available, optimization functions invisibly return data frame one row per result columns containing source destination paths, formatted raw file sizes, compression ratios status notes. return NULL otherwise. Successful API calls also write optimized files disk. following example shows result local image file:
code: library(resmush)  url <- "https://dieghernan.github.io/resmush/img/jpg_example_original.jpg"  resmush_url(url, outfile = "jpg_example_compress.jpg", overwrite = TRUE)  #> ══ resmush summary ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, 178.7 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: size is now 45 Kb (was 178.7 Kb). Saved 133.7 Kb (74.82%). #> Saved result in directory 'C:/user/john_doe/AppData/Local/Temp/'. # Use a low JPEG quality level. resmush_url(   url,   outfile = tempfile(fileext = ".jpg"),   overwrite = TRUE,   qlty = 3 )  #> ══ resmush summary ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, 178.7 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: size is now 2.2 Kb (was 178.7 Kb). Saved 176.4 Kb (98.74%). #> Saved result in directory 'C:/user/john_doe/AppData/Local/Temp/'. png_file <- system.file("extimg/example.png", package = "resmush")  # Copy to a temporary file for this example. tmp_png <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") file.copy(png_file, tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  summary <- resmush_file(tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE)  tibble::as_tibble(summary[, -c(1, 2)]) #>   # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   src_size dest_size compress_ratio notes src_bytes dest_bytes #>   <chr>    <chr>     <chr>          <chr>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 239.9 Kb 70.7 Kb   70.54%         OK       245618      72356

title: Using resmush
text: Several R packages provide image optimization tools: xfun::tinify(): Similar resmush_file() uses TinyPNG requires API key. xfun::optipng(): Compresses local files using OptiPNG. program must installed locally. tinieR package: R interface TinyPNG. tinyimg package (Xie 2026a): Optimizes local PNG JPEG files using Rust libraries. supports lossless PNG optimization via oxipng, optional lossy PNG palette reduction JPEG re-encoding via mozjpeg. optout package: Similar xfun::optipng() options. requires additional local software. Table 1: R packages: comparison image optimization alternatives. Table 2: R packages: supported formats. practice, resmush designed quick image optimization minimal setup, including support online image files formats GIF, BMP TIFF. Packages tinyimg may better fit fully local workflows focused PNG JPEG optimization fine-grained control compression settings.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Diego Hernangómez. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hernangómez D (2026). resmush: Optimize Compress Image Files reSmush.. doi:10.32614/CRAN.package.resmush. https://dieghernan.github.io/resmush/.
code: @Manual{R-resmush,   title = {{resmush}: Optimize and Compress Image Files with {reSmush.it}},   doi = {10.32614/CRAN.package.resmush},   author = {Diego Hernangómez},   year = {2026},   version = {1.0.2},   url = {https://dieghernan.github.io/resmush/},   abstract = {Optimize and compress local and online image files with the reSmush.it API <https://resmush.it/api/>. Process individual files or entire directories. The API is free for personal use, accepts files smaller than 5 MB and supports PNG, JPEG, GIF, BMP and TIFF files.}, }

what: resmush | Optimize and compress image files with reSmush.it
title: resmush | Optimize and compress image files with reSmush.it
text: resmush R package optimizing local online image files, individually entire directories, reSmush.API. API free personal use require API key. reSmush.also available WordPress tools. reSmush.API provides: Optimization without API key. Support PNG, JPEG, GIF, BMP TIFF files. file size limit less 5 MB. pngquant: Removes unnecessary data PNG files preserving full alpha transparency. jpegoptim: Lossless optimization based Huffman table optimization. OptiPNG: PNG optimizer used several online compression tools. package stable maintained best-effort basis. currently prioritize CRAN compatibility, bug fixes regressions new features.

text: Install resmush CRAN :

title: resmush | Optimize and compress image files with reSmush.it
text: Optimize download online JPEG image resmush_url(): () (b) Figure 1: Original image (): 178.7 KB, optimized image (b): 45 KB (compression: 74.8%). Click enlarge. Use qlty argument adjust JPEG quality level. best results, use values 90.  Figure 2: Image visible compression artifacts caused high compression (qlty = 3). results available, optimization functions invisibly return data frame one row per result columns containing source destination paths, formatted raw file sizes, compression ratios status notes. return NULL otherwise. Successful API calls also write optimized files disk. following example shows result local image file:
code: library(resmush)  url <- "https://dieghernan.github.io/resmush/img/jpg_example_original.jpg"  resmush_url(   url,   outfile = "man/figures/jpg_example_compress.jpg",   overwrite = TRUE ) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, 178.7 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: size is now 45 Kb (was 178.7 Kb). Saved 133.7 Kb (74.82%). #> Saved result in directory 'man/figures'. # Use a low JPEG quality level. resmush_url(   url,   outfile = "man/figures/jpg_example_compress_low.jpg",   overwrite = TRUE,   qlty = 3 ) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, 178.7 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: size is now 2.2 Kb (was 178.7 Kb). Saved 176.4 Kb (98.74%). #> Saved result in directory 'man/figures'. png_file <- system.file("extimg/example.png", package = "resmush")  # Copy to a temporary file for this example. tmp_png <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") file.copy(png_file, tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  summary <- resmush_file(tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE)  tibble::as_tibble(summary[, -c(1, 2)]) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   src_size dest_size compress_ratio notes src_bytes dest_bytes #>   <chr>    <chr>     <chr>          <chr>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 239.9 Kb 70.7 Kb   70.54%         OK       245618      72356

what: Remove output files from directories — resmush_clean_dir
title: Remove output files from directories — resmush_clean_dir
text: Use caution. Remove files match suffix one directories. intended clean output files created resmush_file() resmush_dir().

title: Remove output files from directories — resmush_clean_dir
Remove output files from directories — resmush_clean_dir
text: dir character vector directory paths. See path argument list.files(). suffix character string containing suffix pattern used identify files. value interpreted regular expression. default "_resmush", default suffix used resmush_file(). recursive Logical. file search recurse directories?

title: Remove output files from directories — resmush_clean_dir
text: invisible() NULL. Messages list files selected removal.

title: Remove output files from directories — resmush_clean_dir
code: # \donttest{ # Create a temporary file with a suffix to remove. png_file <- system.file("extimg/example.png", package = "resmush") suffix <- "_would_be_removed" tmp_png <- file.path(   tempdir(),   paste0("example", suffix, ".png") )  file.exists(tmp_png) #> [1] FALSE file.copy(png_file, tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  file.exists(tmp_png) #> [1] TRUE  # Run with the default suffix. This should not remove the file. resmush_clean_dir(tempdir()) #> ℹ No files with suffix "_resmush" found in /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs.  file.exists(tmp_png) #> [1] TRUE  # Use the matching suffix to remove the file. resmush_clean_dir(tempdir(), suffix = suffix) #> ℹ Removing 1 file: #> → /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/example_would_be_removed.png  file.exists(tmp_png) #> [1] FALSE # }

what: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
title: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
text: Optimize supported image files one directories reSmush.API. API free personal use accepts files smaller 5 MB.

title: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
code: resmush_dir(   dir,   ext = "\\.(png|jpe?g|bmp|gif|tif)$",   suffix = "_resmush",   overwrite = FALSE,   progress = TRUE,   report = TRUE,   recursive = FALSE,   ... )

title: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
text: dir character vector paths local directories. ext regex matching file extensions optimize. default matches lowercase .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .bmp .tif extensions. suffix character string inserted output file extension. default "_resmush". Therefore, example.png becomes example_resmush.png. Values "", NA NULL equivalent overwrite = TRUE. overwrite Logical. input files overwritten? TRUE, suffix ignored. progress Logical. progress bar displayed? report Logical. summary report displayed console? recursive Logical. file search within dir recursive? See list.files(). ... Arguments passed resmush_file qlty integer 0 100 indicating JPEG quality level. best results, use values 90. argument affects JPEG files. exif_preserve Logical. EXIF metadata preserved? default FALSE. removes .

title: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
text: invisibly returned data frame one row per result columns containing source destination paths, formatted raw file sizes, compression ratios status notes. Returns NULL result available. Successful API calls also write optimized files disk.

title: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
code: # \donttest{ # Copy the example directory. example_dir <- system.file("extimg", package = "resmush") temp_dir <- tempdir() file.copy(example_dir, temp_dir, recursive = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  # Create the destination folder path. dest_folder <- file.path(tempdir(), "extimg")  # Optimize files non-recursively. resmush_dir(dest_folder) #> ℹ Optimizing 2 files. #> 🕐  reSmushing | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□   50% [2ms] | ETA:  0s (1/2 fi… #> 🕐  reSmushing | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  100% [2.1s] | ETA:  0s (2/2 f… #>  #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 2 files, 340.2 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 2 files: size is now 153.8 Kb (was 340.2 Kb). Saved 186.4 Kb (54.79%). #> Saved results in directory /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/extimg. resmush_clean_dir(dest_folder) #> ℹ Removing 2 files: #> → /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/extimg/example_resmush.jpg #> → /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/extimg/example_resmush.png  # Optimize files recursively. summary <- resmush_dir(dest_folder, recursive = TRUE) #> ℹ Optimizing 5 files. #> 🕐  reSmushing | ■■■■■■■□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□   20% [1ms] | ETA:  0s (1/5 fi… #> 🕑  reSmushing | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■□□□□□□□□□□□□   60% [2.3s] | ETA:  2s (3/5 f… #> 🕑  reSmushing | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  100% [3.6s] | ETA:  0s (5/5 f… #>  #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 5 files, 401.7 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 5 files: size is now 173.5 Kb (was 401.7 Kb). Saved 228.2 Kb (56.81%). #> Saved results in directories /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/extimg, #> /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/extimg/top1/nested, /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/extimg/top1, and #> /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/extimg/top2.  # Inspect the returned optimization summary. summary[, -c(1, 2)] #>   src_size dest_size compress_ratio notes src_bytes dest_bytes #> 1 100.4 Kb   83.2 Kb         17.15%    OK    102796      85164 #> 2 239.9 Kb   70.7 Kb         70.54%    OK    245618      72356 #> 3  17.8 Kb      6 Kb         66.48%    OK     18214       6105 #> 4  25.9 Kb    7.7 Kb         70.09%    OK     26499       7926 #> 5  17.8 Kb      6 Kb         66.48%    OK     18214       6105  # Display the PNG output. if (require("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   a_png <- grepl("png$", summary$dest_img)   my_png <- png::readPNG(summary[a_png, ]$dest_img[2])   grid::grid.raster(my_png) }   # Clean up the example files. unlink(dest_folder, force = TRUE, recursive = TRUE) # }

title: Optimize local image files — resmush_file
text: Optimize one local image files reSmush.API. API free personal use accepts files smaller 5 MB.

title: Optimize local image files — resmush_file
code: resmush_file(   file,   suffix = "_resmush",   overwrite = FALSE,   progress = TRUE,   report = TRUE,   qlty = 92,   exif_preserve = FALSE )

title: Optimize local image files — resmush_file
text: file character vector paths local image files. API can optimize PNG, JPEG, GIF, BMP TIFF files. suffix character string inserted output file extension. default "_resmush". Therefore, example.png becomes example_resmush.png. Values "", NA NULL equivalent overwrite = TRUE. overwrite Logical. input files overwritten? TRUE, suffix ignored. progress Logical. progress bar displayed? report Logical. summary report displayed console? qlty integer 0 100 indicating JPEG quality level. best results, use values 90. argument affects JPEG files. exif_preserve Logical. EXIF metadata preserved? default FALSE. removes .

title: Optimize local image files — resmush_file
text: invisibly returned data frame one row per result columns containing source destination paths, formatted raw file sizes, compression ratios status notes. Returns NULL result available. Successful API calls also write optimized files disk. report = TRUE, summary displayed console.

title: Optimize local image files — resmush_file
code: # \donttest{ png_file <- system.file("extimg/example.png", package = "resmush")  # Copy to a temporary file for this example. tmp_png <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")  file.copy(png_file, tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  resmush_file(tmp_png) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 file, 239.9 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 1 file: size is now 70.7 Kb (was 239.9 Kb). Saved 169.2 Kb (70.54%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs.  # Optimize multiple files. jpg_file <- system.file("extimg/example.jpg", package = "resmush") tmp_jpg <- tempfile(fileext = ".jpg")  file.copy(jpg_file, tmp_jpg, overwrite = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  # Display a summary in the console. summary <- resmush_file(c(tmp_png, tmp_jpg)) #> 🕐  reSmushing | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□   50% [1ms] | ETA:  0s (1/2 fi… #> 🕐  reSmushing | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  100% [1.1s] | ETA:  0s (2/2 f… #>  #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 2 files, 340.2 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 2 files: size is now 153.8 Kb (was 340.2 Kb). Saved 186.4 Kb (54.79%). #> Saved results in directory /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs.  # Inspect the returned optimization summary. summary #>                                src_img #> 1 /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/file1b8f6d92b990.png #> 2 /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/file1b8f61050e64.jpg #>                                       dest_img src_size dest_size #> 1 /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/file1b8f6d92b990_resmush.png 239.9 Kb   70.7 Kb #> 2 /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/file1b8f61050e64_resmush.jpg 100.4 Kb   83.2 Kb #>   compress_ratio notes src_bytes dest_bytes #> 1         70.54%    OK    245618      72356 #> 2         17.15%    OK    102796      85164  # Display the PNG output. if (require("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   my_png <- png::readPNG(summary$dest_img[1])   grid::grid.raster(my_png) }   # Adjust the JPEG quality level. resmush_file(tmp_jpg) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 file, 100.4 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 1 file: size is now 83.2 Kb (was 100.4 Kb). Saved 17.2 Kb (17.15%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs. resmush_file(tmp_jpg, qlty = 10) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 file, 100.4 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 1 file: size is now 6.4 Kb (was 100.4 Kb). Saved 94 Kb (93.61%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs. # }

title: Optimize online image files — resmush_url
text: Optimize download one online image files reSmush.API. API free personal use accepts files smaller 5 MB.

title: Optimize online image files — resmush_url
code: resmush_url(   url,   outfile = file.path(tempdir(), basename(url)),   overwrite = FALSE,   progress = TRUE,   report = TRUE,   qlty = 92,   exif_preserve = FALSE )

title: Optimize online image files — resmush_url
text: url character vector URLs pointing hosted image files. API can optimize PNG, JPEG, GIF, BMP TIFF files. outfile character vector paths optimized files stored. default, files created tempdir() basename() file url. outfile must length url. overwrite Logical. existing files outfile overwritten? FALSE, existing paths made unique numeric suffix, example_01.png. progress Logical. progress bar displayed? report Logical. summary report displayed console? qlty integer 0 100 indicating JPEG quality level. best results, use values 90. argument affects JPEG files. exif_preserve Logical. EXIF metadata preserved? default FALSE. removes .

title: Optimize online image files — resmush_url
text: invisibly returned data frame one row per result columns containing source destination paths, formatted raw file sizes, compression ratios status notes. Returns NULL result available. Successful API calls also write optimized files disk. outfile contains duplicate paths, resmush_url() makes unique suffixes _01 _02.

title: Optimize online image files — resmush_url
code: # \donttest{ # Define the base URL. base_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dieghernan/resmush/main/inst/"  png_url <- paste0(base_url, "/extimg/example.png") resmush_url(png_url) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, 239.9 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: size is now 70.7 Kb (was 239.9 Kb). Saved 169.2 Kb (70.54%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs.  # Optimize multiple URLs. jpg_url <- paste0(base_url, "/extimg/example.jpg")  summary <- resmush_url(c(png_url, jpg_url)) #> 🕐  reSmushing | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□   50% [1ms] | ETA:  0s (1/2 UR… #> 🕐  reSmushing | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  100% [1.3s] | ETA:  0s (2/2 U… #>  #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 2 URLs, 340.2 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 2 URLs: size is now 153.8 Kb (was 340.2 Kb). Saved 186.4 Kb (54.79%). #> Saved results in directory /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs.  # Inspect the returned optimization summary. summary #>                                                                              src_img #> 1 https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dieghernan/resmush/main/inst//extimg/example.png #> 2 https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dieghernan/resmush/main/inst//extimg/example.jpg #>                         dest_img src_size dest_size compress_ratio notes #> 1 /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/example_01.png 239.9 Kb   70.7 Kb         70.54%    OK #> 2    /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs/example.jpg 100.4 Kb   83.2 Kb         17.15%    OK #>   src_bytes dest_bytes #> 1    245618      72356 #> 2    102796      85164  # Display the PNG output. if (require("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   my_png <- png::readPNG(summary$dest_img[1])   grid::grid.raster(my_png) }   # Adjust the JPEG quality level. resmush_url(jpg_url) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, 100.4 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: size is now 83.2 Kb (was 100.4 Kb). Saved 17.2 Kb (17.15%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs. resmush_url(jpg_url, qlty = 10) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, 100.4 Kb total. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: size is now 6.4 Kb (was 100.4 Kb). Saved 94 Kb (93.61%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/RtmpT8yuRs. # }

title: resmush 1.0.2
text: Improved testing.

title: resmush 1.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-29 Improved console messages optimization reports file management helpers. Refactored internal code updated function documentation AI assistance.

title: resmush 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-14 Corrected typos. Migrated documentation Quarto. Increased minimum required R version 4.1.0. Released version 1.0.0 indicate package reached mature development state.

title: resmush 0.2.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-12 API downloads now perform HEAD request verify optimized file exists. API requests now include package website HTTP referrer.

title: resmush 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-12-18 Updated documentation.

title: resmush 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-07-19 Removed WebP support API longer supports format.

title: resmush 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-05-22 Updated documentation.

title: resmush 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-02-02 Accepted CRAN. Added DOI https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.10556679. Added png grid packages Suggests examples. Switched httr package httr2 package. resmush_clean_dir() now shows summary message. resmush_dir(), resmush_file() resmush_url() gained overwrite argument. resmush_dir(), resmush_file() resmush_url() gained progress report arguments. add messages progress bars using cli::cli_progress_bar(). resmush_dir(), resmush_file() resmush_url() deprecated verbose argument.

title: resmush 0.0.1
text: Initial version.
